VACANCY PERMIT ENDORSEMENT
In consideration of the additional premium charged, the provisions of
Section 7.3, *Vacancy Exclusion*, of the base policy are hereby modified
as follows:
section_title: VACANCY PERMIT ENDORSEMENT

text:
1. Modification to Section 7.3 – Vacancy Exclusion
a. Extension of Vacancy Period
The sixty (60) consecutive day vacancy period referenced in Section 7.3
is extended to one hundred twenty (120) consecutive days. This extension

applies solely to the vacancy exclusion and does not alter any other
provisions of the base policy.
b. Notification Requirement
The Insured shall provide written notice to the Company within ten (10)
days of the property becoming vacant. Failure to provide such notice
shall void the modification set forth in Subsection 1.a. above.
c. Preservation of Other Terms
All other terms, conditions, exclusions, and limitations of Section 7.3
remain unchanged and continue to apply in full force and effect, except
as expressly modified herein.
section_title: applies solely to the vacancy exclusion and does not alter any other

text:
2. Definitions
For purposes of this endorsement, the term “vacant” shall retain the
meaning assigned in Section 7.3 of the base policy, unless otherwise
modified by this endorsement.
3. Premium Adjustment

The additional premium for this endorsement is calculated based on the
risk exposure associated with the extended vacancy period and is due upon
attachment of this endorsement to the policy.

practice under California Insurance Code § 790.03(h).
Section 4. Mold Disclosure Requirements
4.1. The insurer shall comply with California’s mold disclosure
requirements, as set forth in California Insurance Code § 2071. This
includes:

policy renewal regarding the availability of mold coverage, if any, and
the limitations or exclusions thereto;

b. Disclosing any dollar limitations or exclusions for mold claims in the
policy declarations or endorsements.
4.2. Any policy exclusion or limitation relating to mold must be clearly
and conspicuously stated in the policy, as required by § 2071.2.

SCHEDULED PERSONAL PROPERTY ENDORSEMENT
This endorsement, NX-END-04, amends the Homeowners Policy issued to the
Named Insured, as identified in the Declarations Page, by adding
scheduled coverage for the high-value items listed herein. The provisions
of this endorsement supersede any conflicting terms in Section I –
Property Coverages, particularly Coverage C – Personal Property, to the
extent such terms limit coverage for the scheduled items.
section_title: SCHEDULED PERSONAL PROPERTY ENDORSEMENT

text:
1. SCHEDULED ITEMS
The following items are scheduled for coverage under this endorsement,
with individual coverage limits as specified:
| Item Description | Acquired | Serial/Registration No. | Coverage Limit
| Deductible |
|----------------------|--------------|-----------------------------|----
----------------|----------------|
| 1. Diamond Engagement Ring (2.5 carats, platinum setting) | 03/15/2020
| N/A | $25,000 | $0 |

| 12345678 | $12,000 | $0 |

11/10/2018 | N/A | $18,000 | $0 |
| 4. 14K White Gold Tennis Bracelet (3.8 carats total) | 05/05/2021 | N/A
| $15,000 | $0 |
| 5. Vintage Fender Stratocaster Guitar (1962, Sunburst) | 09/18/2017 |
SN12345678 | $8,500 | $0 |
2. COVERAGE PROVISIONS

2.1 Coverage A – Scheduled Property
Each scheduled item is insured under Coverage A – Scheduled Property for
the full coverage limit stated in the schedule, subject to the following:
(a) The items are covered for all risks of direct physical loss except as
excluded in Section I – Exclusions of the base policy.
(b) Coverage applies worldwide, including while in transit.
(c) The insurer shall not be liable for loss caused by inherent vice,
gradual deterioration, or mechanical breakdown unless resulting from a
covered peril.

2.2 Appraisal Requirement
(a) For items valued at $5,000 or more, the Named Insured must provide a
professional appraisal upon request by the insurer.
(b) The insurer reserves the right to require an updated appraisal every
three (3) years for scheduled items.

2.3 Claims Settlement
(a) In the event of a covered loss, the insurer’s liability shall not
exceed the lesser of:

(i) The cost to repair or replace the item with like kind and quality; or
(ii) The scheduled coverage limit.
(b) The insurer may elect to pay the actual cash value of the item if
repair or replacement is not feasible.

3. EXCLUSIONS
This endorsement does not cover:
(a) Mysterious disappearance unless specifically endorsed.
(b) Loss caused by war, nuclear hazard, or intentional acts by the Named
Insured.
(c) Wear and tear, inherent vice, or mechanical breakdown (unless
resulting from a covered peril).
4. CONDITIONS

4.1 Duties After Loss
The Named Insured must comply with all duties outlined in Section I –
Conditions of the base policy, including prompt notice of loss and
submission of proof of loss.
4.2 Cancellation

This endorsement may be canceled by the insurer or Named Insured with ten
(10) days’ written notice.

text:
1.1 Deductible Applicable to Wind and Hail Losses. In the event of loss
or damage caused by windstorm or hail, the deductible applicable to such

loss shall be separate from and in addition to any other deductible
provided under the policy. The deductible for windstorm or hail losses
shall be two percent (2%) of the limit of liability under Coverage A –
Dwelling.
section_title: loss shall be separate from and in addition to any other deductible

text:
1.2 Calculation of Deductible. The deductible shall be calculated as
follows:
a. The deductible amount shall be two percent (2%) of the Coverage A
limit stated in the Declarations.
b. The deductible shall apply on a per occurrence basis.
c. The deductible shall not be subject to any other deductible or
limitation under the policy.

1.3 Application of Deductible. The deductible shall apply to all loss or
damage caused by windstorm or hail, including but not limited to damage
to the dwelling, other structures, and personal property.
SECTION 2 – FLOOD EXCLUSION AND DISCLAIMER

2.1 Flood Exclusion. Loss or damage caused directly or indirectly by
flood is excluded from coverage under the policy. For purposes of this
endorsement, "flood" means:
a. A general and temporary condition of partial or complete inundation of
normally dry land areas from:
i. The overflow of inland or tidal waters;
ii. The unusual and rapid accumulation or runoff of surface waters from
any source; or
iii. Mudslides or mudflows caused by flooding.
b. Collapse or subsidence of land along the shore of a lake or similar

body of water as a result of erosion or undermining caused by waves or
currents of water exceeding anticipated cyclical levels.

4. Period of Restoration.

SECTION III – EXCLUSIONS
section_title: SECTION III

text:
1. General Exclusions. We will not pay for loss or damage caused directly
or indirectly by any of the following:
a. Ordinance or Law. Loss resulting from the enforcement of any ordinance
or law regulating the construction, repair, or demolition of buildings or
structures.
b. Earth Movement. Loss caused by or resulting from earth movement,
including earthquake, landslide, mudslide, or sinkhole.
c. Governmental Action. Loss caused by or resulting from the destruction,
confiscation, or seizure of Covered Property by order of any governmental
or public authority.
d. Nuclear Hazard. Loss caused by or resulting from nuclear reaction,
radiation, or radioactive contamination.
e. Utility Services. Loss caused by or resulting from the failure of
power, heat, refrigeration supply, or communication services supplied to
the described premises, unless the failure is caused by a Covered Cause
of Loss that occurs on the described premises.
f. War and Military Action. Loss caused by or resulting from war,
including undeclared war, civil war, insurrection, rebellion, or
revolution.

2. Additional Exclusions.
a. Vacancy. If the building where loss or damage occurs has been vacant
for more than 60 consecutive days before the loss or damage, we will not
pay for any loss or damage caused by:
(1) Vandalism;
(2) Sprinkler leakage, unless the sprinkler system was protected against
freezing;
(3) Breakage of building glass;
(4) Water damage;
(5) Theft or attempted theft.
b. Neglect. Loss caused by or resulting from the Insured’s neglect to
reasonably protect Covered Property from further damage.
c. Pollution. Loss caused by or resulting from the discharge, dispersal,
seepage, migration, or release of pollutants unless the discharge,
dispersal, or release is itself caused by a Covered Cause of Loss.

section_title: Section I

text:
1. Flood Coverage Addition
a. In consideration of the premium specified, the following provisions
are added to Section I – Exclusions, paragraph A.3. (Water Damage) of the
Policy Form NX-HO-3:
i. Notwithstanding the foregoing, this Policy is amended to provide

coverage for direct physical loss to the Covered Property described in
Coverages A and B caused by flood, as defined herein.
ii. For purposes of this Endorsement, "flood" means:
(1) A general and temporary condition of partial or complete inundation
of normally dry land areas from:
(a) The overflow of inland or tidal waters;
(b) The unusual and rapid accumulation or runoff of surface waters from
any source;
(c) Mudslides or mudflows on the surface of normally dry land areas, as
section_title: coverage for direct physical loss to the Covered Property described in

text:
these terms are defined under the National Flood Insurance Program
(NFIP); or
(d) The collapse or subsidence of land along the shore of a lake or
section_title: these terms are defined under the National Flood Insurance Program

text:
similar body of water as a result of erosion or undermining caused by
waves or currents of water exceeding anticipated cyclical levels.
(2) The release of water from the failure of a dam or levee, provided
section_title: similar body of water as a result of erosion or undermining caused by

text:
such failure is not caused by an act of negligence or willful misconduct
of the Insured.
section_title: such failure is not caused by an act of negligence or willful misconduct

text:
2. Limits of Liability
a. The most Nexus Insurance Company will pay for loss or damage caused by
flood under Coverages A and B is the lesser of:
i. The Coverage A limit shown on the Declarations Page; or
ii. $250,000 for the Dwelling and $25,000 for Other Structures.

3. Deductible
a. A deductible of $2,500 applies to each flood event. This deductible is
in addition to any other deductible shown on the Declarations Page.
B. Conditions

1. Notice of Loss
a. The Insured shall provide prompt notice to Nexus Insurance Company of
any loss or damage caused by flood, including the date and time the flood
began, if known.

2. Proof of Loss
a. The Insured shall file a proof of loss within 60 days after the date
of loss, unless such time is extended in writing by Nexus Insurance
Company.

3. Governmental Action
a. Any loss caused by flood resulting from governmental action or order
to prevent or mitigate flood damage is excluded.

4. Concurrent Causation
a. If flood is the efficient proximate cause of loss, coverage applies.
If flood is a concurrent cause with an excluded peril, coverage does not
apply.

5. Exclusions
a. This Endorsement does not insure:
i. Loss caused by water that backs up through sewers or drains;
ii. Loss caused by water below the surface of the ground, including water
in a basement or crawl space; or
iii. Loss caused by seepage or leakage of water through a foundation or
retaining wall.
C. Definitions
a. "Flood" is defined in Section A.1.a.ii of this Endorsement.
D. Effective Date and Premium
a. This Endorsement is effective at 12:01 a.m., standard time, at the
Insured location on the date shown on the Declarations Page, subject to
payment of the required premium.
E. Cancellation
a. This Endorsement may be canceled by Nexus Insurance Company or the
Insured in accordance with the Policy provisions.

on the Declarations Page.
